The sections HashMap uses arena allocation so everything gets cleaned up together when the arena is deinitialized, and the JSValues are also stored on the result object so they won't be lost.

I'm realizing there's a potential GC concern with storing JSValues in a heap-allocated HashMap - the GC might not scan it directly. But since the result object lives on the Zig stack and the section objects are children of result, they're reachable through conservative scanning. The HashMap just holds references to the same JSValues already on result, so they're safe even if the HashMap itself isn't scanned. ## Summary: `Bun.INI` implementation

### New files

**`src/interchange/ini.zig`** — Self-contained INI parser with no external dependencies.

**`src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`** — JavaScript bindings.
- `create()` — builds the `{ parse, stringify }` namespace object.
- `parse(input)` — iterates the `ini.Parser`, builds a JS object. Top-level keys go directly on the root; section keys go on a nested sub-object (duplicate section headers are merged via an arena-allocated `StringHashMap`).
- `stringify(input)` — two-pass: first emits root-level scalars as `key=value\n`, then emits each object value as `\n[section]\nkey=value\n` blocks. Arrays and functions are skipped.

**`test/js/bun/ini.test.ts`** — comprehensive test suite (30+ cases covering parse and stringify).
